If the Monroe street bridge is
built in accordance with the &C.
ceptance of plans last night by the
city council another bridge bond
issue of 100000 will be necessary.
This will make a total of 100000
the city will have raised for
bridges in the past two years.


Out of the original issue of $780,
900 the city has the Washington
street bridge complete and the Mis-
sion and Howard street bridges I'M
der construction, with no little
bridge building experience to boot.


The new Monroe bridge will cost
1914 900 according to the city en-
gineer's estimate, with possibly
$4,000 added for purchase of
equipment If the work is done by
day labor. That the work will be
done by day labor is believed to be
certain and the extra equipment
cost will not reach the estimate for
the reason that much of the ma
chinery used on the Washington
bridge can be utilized at Monroe.


Councilman Mohr, who moved
acceptance of the plans last night,
favors day labor with strict prefer
ence for Spokane heads of families
in employment. He will not OBJ
